metric_value: Under $50 million in 1990; growing at 20%-25% in 1991
notes: Aberdeen's estimate; market characterized as small, mixed with client-server in buyer minds

metric_value: Most buyers believe 1994 is when organizations will be fully up to speed with client-server; technology barriers until early 1992 or later
notes: 1994-1995 client-server maturity predicted

metric_value: Client-server matured by mid-1990s as predicted; World Wide Web arrival 1993-1995 accelerated and disrupted transition
notes: Aberdeen's prediction proved largely accurate; web added new dimension not anticipated

metric_value: Over 80% of corporate MIS committed to IBM PC architecture; unwilling to consider Apple for future client-server and front-end projects
notes: Substantial organizational inertia surrounding IBM PC; approved supplier lists invariably include IBM PS/2

metric_value: Apple's market share in this decade unlikely to exceed 15% of business PC market; large MIS shops putting up largest resistance
notes: Long-term prediction from 1991

metric_name: Apple enterprise market share actual outcome
metric_value: macOS desktop market share ~16% in 2025 (all PCs); Apple enterprise presence grew significantly post-iPhone but fragmented MIS pattern persisted
notes: Aberdeen's 15% ceiling proved roughly accurate for traditional enterprise; Apple's consumer success changed the calculus after 2007

metric_value: Only large mainframe site to have implemented frontware application using standard PC; used for flight status display
notes: Delta was Apple success story; Prudential-Bache used proprietary CTOS frontware separately

metric_value: ~5 million IBM 3270 terminals declining at 15%-20% per year through 1993; PC replacement of choice
notes: Gradual divestment due to capital costs of replacing 10,000-20,000 devices at large sites

metric_value: Most frequently mentioned as likely target for client applications; has established itself as a standard
notes: Windows 3.0 released May 1990; rapid enterprise adoption confirmed in interviews

metric_name: IBM OS/2 Presentation Manager enterprise reception
metric_value: Late and slow; used for multithreading or IBM OfficeVision/2 commitments; SAA still 2 years away
notes: OS/2 ultimately failed to displace Windows; Microsoft Windows won the desktop

metric_name: University of Texas Apple installed base
metric_value: Largest Mac site in US: 18,000 sold; 2,000 in administration alone; using SAG Natural Connection for frontware
notes: UT Austin as flagship Apple enterprise success; MIS writes applications to 3270; client handles front end

metric_name: Novell NetWare dominance and Apple connectivity gap
metric_value: Novell NetWare is dominant LAN OS; Mac-NetWare 286 support unknown to MIS; Netware 386 Mac connectivity delayed
notes: Aberdeen: Apple must be widely known as having excellent connectivity to Novell LANs

metric_value: Aberdeen concerned Apple planning fewer than 25 frontware pilots in 1991; Fortune 1000 needs 1991 wake-up call
notes: Recommended broad-based seminars rather than targeted vertical marketing due to cross-industry nature

metric_name: Apple quality reputation as enterprise asset
metric_value: Apple has favorable reputation as quality company; buyers seeking flight to safety with highest quality, financially strong, reputable suppliers
notes: Aberdeen identifies Apple's quality reputation as 'understated asset'

metric_value: $175M S&P budget; planning native APPC applications in 1991; productivity tools 1992; common use 1994
notes: Following IBM two-tier topology model; 386SX PC, Sun Sparc or RS/6000 as client platforms

metric_value: By 1994, USAA plans $5M+ rewrite of core customer service application using 5,000 client workstations; PS/2 Model 55SX with OS/2
notes: 10,000 users at USAA; Apple OK on approved list but neutral; IBM account team 'all over USAA'

metric_value: Planning to replace 8,000 broker workstations; three recommended platforms under budgetary consideration; Unisys/CTOS expected to lose contract
notes: Everyone in brokerage industry has gone IBM PCs; Apple not a viable candidate

metric_value: Frontware and client-server are mutually exclusive; MIS will either retrofit mainframe app with frontware or rearchitect using client-server
notes: Application re-engineering using frontware competes with new-application development using client-server for same 10-20% of MIS budget

metric_value: At 10%-20% of sites, Apple is established supplier with healthy installed base; other 80% have virtually zero Apple visibility
notes: No middle ground; binary: either recognized player or totally ignored. Fragmented market prevents targeted marketing.

metric_name: Software AG Natural Connection joint marketing recommendation
metric_value: Aberdeen recommends evaluation of expanded joint marketing with Software AG of Natural Connection for Macintosh for client CASE tools and 4GL
notes: UT Austin using SAG on Macintosh successfully as production frontware platform

metric_name: Dealer channel limitation for Apple enterprise sales
metric_value: Dealers taking orders for IBM PCs rather than educating market about Apple; dealers have few incentives to sell Apple when customer says IBM PC
notes: Even large organizations buy IBM PCs through same dealer channels that sell Apple

metric_name: SNA SDLC departmental gateway topology emergence
metric_value: Aberdeen anticipates rapid growth in market for departmental communications gateways running SNA SDLC to IBM mainframe; potential cost-per-seat advantage for Apple
notes: Gateway vendors: Avatar, DCA, Tri-Data cited

metric_value: No specific client-server plans; 20,000+ 3270s; database checking requirement makes frontware inappropriate; IBM mandatory for AD/Cycle
notes: $25M S&P budget; 5-year timeline for any client-server; IBM has 15 resident personnel on-site

metric_name: Unix workstations for corporate MIS desktops
metric_value: Unix workstations are non-starters for corporate MIS desktops; Unix servers attracting increasing interest for price-performance advantages particularly for RDBMS decision support
notes: Sun Sparc and RS/6000 mentioned as possible server platforms at Merrill Lynch

metric_name: Apple iPhone and enterprise market disruption
metric_value: Apple launched iPhone 2007 and iPad 2010; consumer device success fundamentally changed enterprise adoption patterns; BYOD eliminated historical barriers
notes: Aberdeen did not anticipate consumer device strategy as enterprise entry point; report focused purely on MIS decisions
